# Software Requirements Specification

## RAG Refinement System — Smart Context RAG Optimizer

**Document Version:** 1.0
**Date:** 2026-06-06
**Status:** DOCUMENTATION APPROVED (consensus BINARY gate, 0 open items — see Section 6 Change Log and `docs/phase-5-documentation/doc_verdict.json`)
**Standard:** IEEE-830-style
**Author:** business-analyst-agent + product-manager-agent (Phase 5 Blueprint Documentation)
**Traceability rule:** Every functional requirement in Section 3.1 traces to a PRD FR-NNN; every non-functional requirement in Section 3.2 traces to a PRD NFR-NNN. No requirement is introduced that is not already specified in `PRD.md` v2.0. Architecture references trace to `docs/phase-1-architecture/hld.md` (HLD) and `docs/phase-1-api-contracts/openapi.yaml`.

---

## 1. Purpose

The RAG Refinement System is a document-structure-aware retrieval layer that improves the accuracy, speed, and cost of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) over structured documents (PRD §1). Standard RAG searches an entire corpus for every query, wasting tokens and introducing hallucination-inducing noise. This system introduces a Table-of-Contents (TOC) based **Router Agent** that first identifies *which sections* of a document are relevant to a query, then performs targeted vector retrieval *only from those sections* — "scope before search" (HLD §1).

This SRS specifies the software requirements for the two product surfaces defined in the PRD (§7) and architected in the HLD (§1.1):

1. **Personal / Developer Tool** — a web SPA where users upload PDFs and receive cited, streamed answers with an interpretable routing explanation (PRD Angle 1).
2. **Enterprise Refinement API** — a routing-only REST layer that an existing RAG integrates to narrow its retrieval scope before vector search (PRD Angle 2). `POST /v1/route` performs no generation and never calls the generation LLM (HLD §7.2 invariant; AGREED CONTRACT).

The intended audience is the implementation, test, security, and operations teams executing Phases B through G, plus enterprise integrators and DPDP data-subject stakeholders.

---

## 2. Scope

### 2.1 In Scope

- PDF ingestion: parse, TOC extraction (Scenarios A/B/C per ADR-4), section-aware chunking, embedding, and dual-store indexing (Qdrant chunk vectors + PostgreSQL L1/L2 structure) — PRD FR-001..FR-004.
- TOC-based query routing via a LangGraph 1.0 router (Claude 3 Haiku, ADR-1/ADR-3) emitting `{relevant_sections[], page_ranges[], confidence[], fallback, routing_time_ms}` — PRD FR-005.
- Targeted retrieval filtered to router-selected `section_id`s, with graceful fallback to full-document RAG — PRD FR-006, FR-009.
- Personal-tool Q&A: chat interface, source citations, streamed answers, confidence display, and a "why did you look here?" explainability panel — PRD FR-007, FR-008, FR-011, FR-012, FR-018.
- Enterprise routing-only endpoint `POST /v1/route` — PRD FR-010.
- Should/Nice features per PRD §9.2/§9.3 — hybrid search, multi-document routing, query rewriting, optional Cohere reranking, OCR fallback, feedback capture, analytics dashboard, SDKs, fine-tuning, multilingual support, and document library — PRD FR-013..FR-024.
- Compliance and data-handling: DPDP erasure/access endpoints, no-retention mode, India data-residency, and `x-pii` flagging — PRD FR-025..FR-029.
- Non-functional requirements: performance, accuracy, reliability, scalability, security (incl. OWASP LLM Top-10), observability, API contract, interpretability, i18n, availability, compliance, and authentication — PRD NFR-001..NFR-015.

### 2.2 Out of Scope

The non-goals are inherited verbatim from PRD §9.5 and are restated in Section 5.

### 2.3 Definitions and References

Terminology follows PRD Appendix B (Glossary): RAG, TOC, Router Agent, Hierarchical RAG, Agentic RAG, Hallucination, Chunk, Confidence Score, Pseudo-TOC, WSJF, Kano, DPDP, RTM. Authoritative references: `PRD.md` v2.0; `docs/phase-1-architecture/hld.md`; `docs/phase-4-reconciliation/hld_v3.md`; `docs/phase-1-api-contracts/openapi.yaml`; ADR-1..ADR-10 and the AGREED CONTRACTS in `docs/execution/_common_context.md`.

---

## 3. Requirements

### 3.1 Functional Requirements

> Each SRS-FR restates a PRD FR-NNN in "The system SHALL" voice and records its PRD trace, HLD/API trace, and priority (P0/P1/P2 from PRD §9). No FR exists here without a PRD trace.

| SRS-FR | Requirement (The system SHALL …) | PRD Trace | HLD / API Trace | Priority |
|--------|----------------------------------|-----------|-----------------|----------|
| **SRS-FR-001** | accept PDF upload and extract text + structure. | FR-001 | HLD §3.1; `POST /v1/documents` | P0 |
| **SRS-FR-002** | extract a native TOC from bookmarks (Scenario A) and generate an LLM pseudo-TOC fallback (Scenarios B/C). | FR-002 | HLD §3.1, ADR-4; `GET /v1/documents/{id}/toc` | P0 |
| **SRS-FR-003** | build a 3-level Document → Section → Chunk hierarchy with no chunk crossing a section boundary. | FR-003 | HLD §4.1; `TocEntry` | P0 |
| **SRS-FR-004** | embed chunks and store them in the vector DB with `section_id`/`doc_id`/`page` payload. | FR-004 | HLD §4.3, ADR-2/ADR-6 | P0 |
| **SRS-FR-005** | route a query to relevant sections via a LangGraph router emitting `{relevant_sections[], page_ranges[], confidence[], fallback, routing_time_ms}`. | FR-005 | HLD §3.2, §7.1, ADR-1/ADR-3; `RouteResponse` | P0 |
| **SRS-FR-006** | perform targeted retrieval filtered to router-selected `section_id`s. | FR-006 | HLD §4.3, §7.5, OAQ-3 | P0 |
| **SRS-FR-007** | provide a Q&A chat interface for the personal tool. | FR-007 | HLD §1.1, ADR-9; `POST /v1/answer` | P0 |
| **SRS-FR-008** | display source citations (section title + page range) for every answer, each cited page within a router-selected section. | FR-008 | HLD §6 (interval tree), §7.3; `Citation` | P0 |
| **SRS-FR-009** | fall back to full-document RAG when all section confidences are below threshold. | FR-009 | HLD §3.2, OAQ-6, ADR-3; `RouteResponse.fallback` | P0 |
| **SRS-FR-010** | expose a routing-only REST endpoint `POST /v1/route` (request/response, no generation). | FR-010 | HLD §7.2 invariant, ADR-5/ADR-7 | P1 |
| **SRS-FR-011** | display routing confidence values to users. | FR-011 | HLD §7.1; `RoutingSummary.confidence` | P1 |
| **SRS-FR-012** | provide a "why did you look here?" explainability panel listing selected sections, scores, page ranges, and rationale. | FR-012 | HLD §7.1 (`rationale`), hld_v3 §R1; `RoutingSummary.rationale` | P1 |
| **SRS-FR-013** | support hybrid search (TOC routing + BM25 keyword + vector). | FR-013 | HLD §3.2 (retrieve nodes) | P1 |
| **SRS-FR-014** | support querying across multiple related documents. | FR-014 | HLD OAQ-4 (map-reduce); `RouteRequest.document_ids[]` | P1 |
| **SRS-FR-015** | rewrite vague queries before routing. | FR-015 | HLD §3.2 (pre-router step) | P1 |
| **SRS-FR-016** | optionally apply Cohere Rerank-3 post-route, toggleable per request. | FR-016 | HLD §3.2 (rerank node), ADR-8; `*.rerank` | P1 |
| **SRS-FR-017** | support scanned/image PDFs via OCR fallback (Azure Document Intelligence). | FR-017 | HLD §3.1 (P3), ADR-4; `IngestRequest.ocr` | P1 |
| **SRS-FR-018** | stream answers token-by-token via SSE, ending with a final event carrying `{answer, citations[], routing{}}`. | FR-018 | HLD §7.3; `SseAnswerStream`, `AnswerFinalEvent` | P1 |
| **SRS-FR-019** | capture user like/dislike feedback to improve routing. | FR-019 | PRD §9.3 (Phase 4 roadmap) | P2 |
| **SRS-FR-020** | provide a dashboard of token savings, accuracy, and usage analytics. | FR-020 | PRD §9.3; HLD observability (LangSmith) | P2 |
| **SRS-FR-021** | provide Python/JavaScript SDKs and published API docs. | FR-021 | PRD §9.3; openapi.yaml (contract source) | P2 |
| **SRS-FR-022** | support domain-specific router fine-tuning. | FR-022 | PRD §9.3; ADR-1 (deferred) | P2 |
| **SRS-FR-023** | support multilingual documents via a multilingual embedding fallback (BGE-M3). | FR-023 | HLD ADR-6; `IngestRequest` | P2 |
| **SRS-FR-024** | provide a user document library / management surface. | FR-024 | HLD §4; `GET /v1/documents`, `GET /v1/documents/{id}` | P2 |
| **SRS-FR-025** | provide `DELETE /v1/documents/{id}` to erase a document and all derived chunks/sections (DPDP erasure). | FR-025 | HLD §7.4, OAQ-2; `ErasureReceipt` | P1 |
| **SRS-FR-026** | provide `GET /v1/documents/{id}/data` returning the personal data held for a document (DPDP access). | FR-026 | HLD §7.4; `DataAccessExport` | P1 |
| **SRS-FR-027** | support a no-retention mode that purges chunks, sections, and raw upload after the answer is returned. | FR-027 | HLD §7.4, OAQ-1; `*.no_retention` | P1 |
| **SRS-FR-028** | support an India data-residency configuration for tenant data. | FR-028 | HLD §4.2, OAQ-5; `IngestRequest.residency_region` | P1 |
| **SRS-FR-029** | flag PII fields with `x-pii` in the OpenAPI schema and access responses. | FR-029 | HLD §4.2 (`pii_flags`); `x-pii`, `PiiField` | P1 |

### 3.2 Non-Functional Requirements

> Each SRS-NFR restates a PRD NFR-NNN and records its trace and architectural mechanism (HLD §9). The regulatory mandates required by this phase — DPDP data-minimization + data-subject-rights, RPwD/APCA accessibility, CERT-In 6-hour incident reporting, and IT Act reasonable security — are folded into the relevant NFRs below and do not introduce requirements absent from the PRD; they elaborate PRD NFR-007 (security), NFR-011 (interpretability/accessibility), and NFR-014 (compliance) and PRD §16.

| SRS-NFR | Category | Requirement | PRD Trace | HLD / Mechanism |
|---------|----------|-------------|-----------|-----------------|
| **SRS-NFR-001** | Performance — Routing | The router LLM call SHALL complete in 100–300 ms; cached TOC lookup SHALL be < 10 ms. | NFR-001 | HLD §9; Claude 3 Haiku + LRU TOC cache (§6) |
| **SRS-NFR-002** | Performance — Overhead | Total added overhead vs. standard RAG SHALL be ≤ +200 ms median. | NFR-002 | HLD §9 latency budget |
| **SRS-NFR-003** | Accuracy — Routing | Router correct-section recall SHALL be ≥ 85% (MVP) / ≥ 95% (6-mo). | NFR-003 | HLD §9; confidence-thresholded top-K + golden eval set |
| **SRS-NFR-004** | Accuracy — Answer | Answer hallucination rate SHALL be < 15% (MVP). | NFR-004 | HLD §9; scope-before-search noise reduction + citation grounding |
| **SRS-NFR-005** | Reliability | Router fallback rate SHALL be < 20% (MVP) / < 10% (6-mo). | NFR-005 | HLD §9; confidence gate + PSI drift monitoring |
| **SRS-NFR-006** | Scalability | The routing path SHALL be designed for horizontal scale to 10K–500K+ calls/month; MVP ships at low scale. | NFR-006 | HLD §10 (Little's Law); stateless async API |
| **SRS-NFR-007** | Security | User documents (PII), provider API keys, and tenant data SHALL be protected; all secrets via 12-factor env / secret manager, never in code/repo. A CERT-In-format 6-hour incident-reporting hook SHALL fire on security events, and stored data SHALL meet IT Act "reasonable security practices" (encryption in transit, access control, audit logging). | NFR-007 | HLD §8; PRD §16.1 (CERT-In 6h), §16.2 (SOC2-ready evidence) |
| **SRS-NFR-008** | Security — LLM | The router/answer path SHALL be hardened against the OWASP LLM Top-10 (prompt injection, corpus poisoning, embedding inversion). | NFR-008 | HLD §8 STRIDE + OWASP LLM map |
| **SRS-NFR-009** | Observability | All routing/generation runs SHALL be traced in LangSmith; each service SHALL expose `/health` and `/ready`. | NFR-009 | HLD §9; `GET /health`, `GET /ready` |
| **SRS-NFR-010** | API Contract | Every endpoint SHALL conform to the Phase 1.5 OpenAPI 3.1 spec; errors SHALL use RFC-7807 problem+json. | NFR-010 | HLD §7, ADR-5; openapi.yaml `Problem` |
| **SRS-NFR-011** | Interpretability & Accessibility | Every answer SHALL be explainable (selected sections, scores, page ranges, rationale). Personal-tool interpretability UI SHALL meet RPwD-Act-aligned accessibility, rendering routing meters/panels at APCA Lc ≥ 60 on body text and conveying state by label+icon+bar (not color alone). | NFR-011 | HLD §7.1; component_library.md (ConfidenceMeter/ExplainabilityPanel APCA Lc≥60) |
| **SRS-NFR-012** | Internationalization | Non-English documents SHALL be supported via the BGE-M3 multilingual embedding fallback. | NFR-012 | HLD ADR-6 |
| **SRS-NFR-013** | Availability | Each service SHALL support graceful degradation to full-doc RAG on router/dependency failure. | NFR-013 | HLD §3.2 fallback branch, ADR-3; `503` → fallback |
| **SRS-NFR-014** | Compliance | The system SHALL meet DPDP Act 2023 obligations — data-minimization (no-retention mode, India residency) and data-subject rights (erasure, access) — and produce SOC2-readiness evidence. | NFR-014 | HLD §7.4, §9; PRD §16.1 (DPDP §4/§8), §16.2 |
| **SRS-NFR-015** | Auth | The enterprise API SHALL authenticate via API keys (with rotation); the personal tool via OAuth2/JWT bearer. | NFR-015 | HLD ADR-7; openapi.yaml `apiKey`/`bearerAuth` |

#### 3.2.1 Regulatory Mapping Note (elaborating NFR-007/011/014)

| Mandate | Realized by SRS requirement(s) | PRD anchor |
|---------|--------------------------------|------------|
| **DPDP Act 2023 — data minimization** | SRS-FR-027 (no-retention), SRS-FR-028 (India residency), SRS-NFR-014 | PRD §16.1, FR-027/028 |
| **DPDP Act 2023 — data-subject rights** | SRS-FR-025 (erasure §8), SRS-FR-026 (access §8), SRS-FR-029 (`x-pii` §4) | PRD §16.1, FR-025/026/029 |
| **RPwD Act / APCA accessibility** | SRS-NFR-011 (APCA Lc≥60, non-color state) | PRD NFR-011; component_library.md |
| **CERT-In 6-hour incident reporting** | SRS-NFR-007 (6h reporting hook) | PRD §16.1 (NFR-007) |
| **IT Act reasonable security** | SRS-NFR-007 (encryption/access-control/audit), SRS-NFR-008 (LLM hardening) | PRD §16.1/§16.3, NFR-007/008 |

No mandate above introduces a requirement absent from the PRD; each maps onto an existing PRD FR/NFR.

---

## 4. Acceptance Criteria

> One acceptance criterion (AC) per functional requirement. ACs for FR-002, FR-005, FR-008, FR-009, FR-025, FR-027 are aligned with the BDD Gherkin scenarios in PRD §12; all others derive directly from the PRD FR statement and the API contract.

| AC | For SRS-FR | Acceptance Criterion |
|----|-----------|----------------------|
| **AC-001** | SRS-FR-001 | Given a valid PDF upload to `POST /v1/documents`, the system returns `IngestResponse{doc_id, toc, ingest_status, deduplicated}` with extracted text+structure; identical re-upload returns `200` with the same `doc_id` and `deduplicated=true`. |
| **AC-002** | SRS-FR-002 | Given a PDF with embedded bookmarks, a TOC is produced from `get_toc()` with `level, title, page_start, page_end`; given a PDF with heading-styled text but no bookmarks, a pseudo-TOC mapping section title → page range is generated (PRD §12 FR-002). |
| **AC-003** | SRS-FR-003 | After ingestion, every chunk carries exactly one `section_id`, and no chunk spans two sections; the L1→L2→L3 hierarchy is queryable via `GET /v1/documents/{id}/toc`. |
| **AC-004** | SRS-FR-004 | Each ingested chunk is embedded and stored as a Qdrant point with payload `{chunk_id, section_id, doc_id, page}` (+ `tenant_id`). |
| **AC-005** | SRS-FR-005 | Given a TOC containing "Warranty & Support" (pp.142–148) and the query "warranty period for the motor?", the router returns that section with confidence ≥ 0.7, `page_ranges` includes [142,148], `fallback=false`, and `routing_time_ms` ∈ [100,300] (PRD §12 FR-005). |
| **AC-006** | SRS-FR-006 | Targeted retrieval issues a Qdrant search with payload filter `section_id IN (router-selected)` AND `tenant_id = caller`; no chunk outside the selected sections is returned on the scoped path. |
| **AC-007** | SRS-FR-007 | A user can submit a question through the personal-tool chat interface and receive a streamed assistant response via `POST /v1/answer`. |
| **AC-008** | SRS-FR-008 | Every generated answer's response includes `citations[{section_title, page_start, page_end}]`, and each cited page falls within a router-selected section (interval-tree validated, HLD §6; PRD §12 FR-008). |
| **AC-009** | SRS-FR-009 | Given all section confidences < 0.5, the system performs full-document retrieval and the response indicates `fallback=true` (PRD §12 FR-009). |
| **AC-010** | SRS-FR-010 | `POST /v1/route` returns `RouteResponse` (routing JSON only) and performs no generation; the graph terminates after the confidence gate (no generation LLM call). |
| **AC-011** | SRS-FR-011 | The personal tool displays a numeric/labelled routing-confidence value derived from `routing.confidence[]` (scalar = `max(confidence[])`, hld_v3 §R2). |
| **AC-012** | SRS-FR-012 | The explainability panel renders `routing.rationale` prose plus a considered-sections list (including rejected sections) with per-section score, title, and page range, resolved by joining `routing.sections[i]`/`confidence[i]` against the loaded TOC (hld_v3 §R1). |
| **AC-013** | SRS-FR-013 | A hybrid-search request fuses TOC-routing scope with BM25 keyword and vector results, and returns a ranked result set scoped to the routed sections. |
| **AC-014** | SRS-FR-014 | Given `document_ids[]` (≤25), the router fans out per-document, merges results with a global confidence sort, and respects `max_sections` (HLD OAQ-4). |
| **AC-015** | SRS-FR-015 | A vague query is rewritten into a clarified form before routing, and the rewritten query is the one routed. |
| **AC-016** | SRS-FR-016 | With `rerank=true`, Cohere Rerank-3 reorders retrieved chunks post-route; with `rerank=false` (default) no reranking occurs. |
| **AC-017** | SRS-FR-017 | A scanned/image PDF (or `ocr=true`) is processed via Azure Document Intelligence OCR and yields extractable text + a TOC or `fallback_only`. |
| **AC-018** | SRS-FR-018 | `POST /v1/answer` emits repeated `event: token` (TokenEvent) messages then a terminal `event: final` (AnswerFinalEvent) carrying `{answer, citations[], routing{}}`; mid-stream errors arrive as `event: error` → `Problem`. |
| **AC-019** | SRS-FR-019 | A user can submit like/dislike feedback on an answer, and the feedback is persisted against the `query_id`. |
| **AC-020** | SRS-FR-020 | A dashboard surfaces token-savings, accuracy, and usage metrics sourced from LangSmith traces. |
| **AC-021** | SRS-FR-021 | Published Python and JavaScript SDKs call the documented endpoints, and API docs are generated from `openapi.yaml`. |
| **AC-022** | SRS-FR-022 | A domain-specific fine-tuned router variant can be selected and produces routing output in the same `RouteResponse` shape. |
| **AC-023** | SRS-FR-023 | A non-English document is embedded via BGE-M3 and is routable/retrievable in its source language. |
| **AC-024** | SRS-FR-024 | `GET /v1/documents` returns a tenant-scoped paginated `DocumentListResponse`; `GET /v1/documents/{id}` returns owned `Document` metadata and is IDOR-guarded (403 on cross-tenant). |
| **AC-025** | SRS-FR-025 | Given an ingested document, an authorized `DELETE /v1/documents/{id}` tombstones it (immediately invisible), removes its sections + chunk vectors via the erasure cascade, and a subsequent `GET` returns 404 (PRD §12 FR-025). |
| **AC-026** | SRS-FR-026 | `GET /v1/documents/{id}/data` returns a `DataAccessExport` containing the document, sections, and a `pii_fields[]` inventory of `x-pii`-flagged fields. |
| **AC-027** | SRS-FR-027 | With `no_retention=true`, after the answer/response the raw upload, chunks, and sections are purged and no document data persists in Qdrant or PostgreSQL (PRD §12 FR-027). |
| **AC-028** | SRS-FR-028 | A tenant flagged `residency_region=IN` has its document data stored on India-region Qdrant/Postgres/object storage. |
| **AC-029** | SRS-FR-029 | PII-bearing fields are annotated `x-pii: true` in the OpenAPI schema and enumerated in the `DataAccessExport.pii_fields[]` of the access response. |

---

## 5. Out of Scope

The following are explicitly out of scope, inherited verbatim from PRD §9.5 (Non-Goals):

- Building a full LLM or foundation model.
- Replacing LangChain or LlamaIndex entirely.
- Real-time streaming document ingestion.
- Image/video/audio content analysis.
- Full enterprise CRM/DMS integration in Phase 1.
- No financial/health *regulatory certification* scope in MVP (legal/healthcare/finance remain named target verticals, so answer faithfulness is treated as safety-critical per PRD §9.5/§18.4).

Additionally out of scope for this SRS document (deferred to later phases per HLD §1.3): implementation code (Phases B), detailed test cases (Phase D), the security audit report (Phase F), and production deployment configuration (Phase G).

---
